correction.summary: Earlier MiniMax compiled/AOT throughput runs around 73 output tok/s are now treated as speed-only diagnostics, not quality-valid benchmark results.
correction.reason: The archived current-best quality smoke contains token 0/NUL output even though the summary file did not preserve quality_checks and was previously interpreted as passing.

root_cause_progress.finding: Default compiled and stock-compiled vLLM/XPU MiniMax paths produce NaN logits and token 0/NUL output.
The non-Inductor path is also corrupted unless cudagraph_num_of_warmups is forced to 0.
Final hidden-state clone/materialization did not repair the compiled path.

quality_valid_fallback.command_shape: vllm bench throughput random p512/n1536, batch=1

engine_flags.commandSnippet: vllm bench throughput --backend vllm --model /mnt/fast-ai/llm-models/minimax-m2.7-int4-autoround --dtype float16 --tensor-parallel-size 4 --max-model-len 2048 --max-num-batched-tokens 512 --max-num-seqs 1 --dataset-name random --random-input-len 512 --random-output-len 1536 --num-prompts 1 --async-engine --block-size 256 --no-enable-prefix-caching --compilation-config '{"mode":0,"cudagraph_num_of_warmups":0}'

quality_smoke.text_prefix: Paris.\n\nThe capital of Germany is Berlin.

next_steps: Treat >60 tok/s target as still open for quality-valid MiniMax.
Focus on isolating the compiled NaN path inside vLLM/XPU/Inductor rather than promoting compiled throughput.
Try disabling compile ranges so prefill stays eager while decode experiments remain isolated.
Instrument compile warmup state and determine why cudagraph_num_of_warmups=1 corrupts mode=NONE decode.
Use the 20.74 tok/s quality-valid fallback as the baseline for future honest comparisons.
